questions.question: What four requirements make video generation much harder than images?
options: Spatiotemporal compression (a 3D VAE encoding videos, not frames), temporal coherence (consistent content/lighting/identity over seconds), a 10-100x compute budget, and multi-signal conditioning (text, first-frame image, audio, video)
questions.explanation: A 10s 1080p clip is ~1.5GB raw — pixel-space diffusion is infeasible. The architecture that solved it: a Diffusion Transformer over spatiotemporal patches with the same DDPM loss.

questions.question: How does a video become a token sequence for the DiT?
options: A 3D VAE compresses to a [T, H, W, C] latent, split into patches with 3D positional embeddings (time + y + x) — a 10-second 1080p video becomes roughly 20,000-100,000 patches
Frames are captioned then tokenized as text
Audio waveforms are interleaved per frame
questions.explanation: The 3D VAE is the video analog of the image VAE in latent diffusion: learned spatiotemporal compression that makes the transformer's sequence length tractable.

questions.question: Why is attention usually factorized into spatial + temporal?
options: Spatial attention within each frame plus temporal attention across frames at the same location is far cheaper — full 3D attention costs 16-100x more and is reserved for low resolutions or research
questions.explanation: With up to 100k patches, full pairwise attention explodes. Factorization is the standard compute compromise, same spirit as separable convolutions.

questions.question: Why did dense re-captioning matter so much for Sora-class models?
options: Training used GPT-generated dense captions averaging ~200 tokens per clip — rich text supervision via a large encoder (T5-XXL) is what makes long, specific prompts actually steer the video
questions.explanation: The same lesson as DALL-E 3: caption quality is a first-order training variable. Compute is the other gate — even small research runs need 10,000+ GPU hours; Sora-scale is 100,000+.

questions.question: What did the 2024-2025 open-weights video stack look like?
options: CogVideoX (first open 5B-scale), HunyuanVideo (13B, open SOTA late 2024), Mochi-1 (most permissive license), and WAN 2.2 (strongest open model mid-2025) — roughly 12 months behind the closed frontier (Sora, Veo, Kling, Runway)
Open models led the closed ones
questions.explanation: The closed-open gap in video mirrors early text-to-image: capability arrives closed (Sora proved it Feb 2024), and open weights follow about a year later at smaller scale.
